cases.instructions:
Implement gitmoot issue #843: external-coordinator job grouping (`--workflow`), coordinator journal (`gitmoot workflow note`), and the memory hook.

You work in /root/gitmoot-wt-843 on branch feat/843-workflow-grouping (current main @ 8c83e21). Repo: jerryfane/gitmoot (Go). Sources of truth: issue #843 AND its tri-model panel synthesis comment (read both: `gh` is unavailable to you — the full text is reproduced below in essentials). Do NOT run git commit/push/gh — edit files only.

## DESIGN (final — panel-converged + coordinator decisions)

### 1. Storage: payload + denormalized indexed column (payload-only is DISQUALIFIED — 48.7MB of payloads measured on the live DB)
- `WorkflowID string \`json:"workflow_id,omitempty"\`` on workflow.JobRequest AND JobPayload (internal/workflow/mailbox.go, next to the Effort field pattern). Byte-identical serialization when absent (round-trip test).
- Byte-appended migration (the tail is FREE — no open PRs): `ALTER TABLE jobs ADD COLUMN workflow_id TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';` + `CREATE INDEX idx_jobs_workflow_id ON jobs(workflow_id) WHERE workflow_id != '';` (partial — zero write cost for unlabeled jobs) + the notes table (below) in the SAME migration entry.
- Derive the column from payload at ALL job insert paths (CreateJob/CreateJobWithEvent/CreateExternalJob — mirror how root_id is derived, internal/db/store.go ~2476-2556).

### 2. Label rules
Slug grammar `^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$` (chat.go slugRe precedent), max 64 chars, global scope (not repo-scoped). Validate at the mailbox enqueue chokepoint (beside validateJobRequest) AND at CLI parse for a friendly early error. Label reuse across time is ACCEPTED for v1 — `workflow list` sorts by last activity and prints first/last timestamps so reuse is visible.

### 3. CLI flag plumbing
`--workflow <label>` (and `--workflow=`) on agent ask + run/review/implement + orchestrate: parseAgentAskOptions and parseAgentRunOptions (mirror the --effort wiring exactly, internal/cli/agent.go), threaded via localAgentDispatchRequest (agent_dispatch.go) onto the JobRequest for BOTH the normal and the permission-blocked enqueue paths. Also accept it on the externally-driven session-job path (`gitmoot job open` — find where OpenExternalJob builds its request) so coordinator-run session work groups too. Update every usage string that shows [--effort effort] to also show [--workflow id].

### 4. Engine tree inheritance (CRITICAL, from the panel)
`orchestrate --workflow` must label the WHOLE tree: every child/continuation JobRequest construction site in internal/workflow/engine.go (the ~7 sites that set `RootJobID:` — grep them fresh, line numbers drift) copies parentPayload.WorkflowID. AND add WorkflowID to `payloadMatchesRequest` (engine.go ~5208-5223) — deterministic continuation IDs must not treat differently-grouped requests as equivalent. Group membership = all inherited tree members.

### 5. Notes table + `gitmoot workflow` command family
- Table (same migration entry): `CREATE TABLE workflow_notes (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, workflow_id TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '', body TEXT NOT NULL, repo TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '', memory_observation_id INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);` + `CREATE INDEX idx_workflow_notes_wid ON workflow_notes(workflow_id, created_at, id);`
- Store API in a NEW file internal/db/workflow_store.go: InsertWorkflowNote, ListWorkflowNotes(workflow, limit), ListWorkflowSummaries (ONE indexed SQL: `SELECT workflow_id, COUNT(*), per-state counts (SUM CASE), COALESCE(SUM(input_tokens),0), COALESCE(SUM(output_tokens),0), MIN(created_at), MAX(updated_at) FROM jobs WHERE workflow_id != '' GROUP BY workflow_id`), ListJobsByWorkflow (indexed, ordered created_at,id — do NOT load payloads for list; select the columns needed).
- CLI in a NEW file internal/cli/workflow_journal.go (internal/cli/workflow.go already holds status/goal code; the top-level command name `workflow` is FREE in root.go — register it):
  - `gitmoot workflow list [--json]` — label, job counts by state, note count, token totals (label "best-effort"), first/last activity; sorted by last activity.
  - `gitmoot workflow show <label> [--json] [--limit N]` — jobs + notes merge-interleaved chronologically (deterministic kind+id tie-break for equal timestamps); per-group token rollup via the dedicated aggregate (NOT SumJobTokensByRoot — ungrouped jobs are SELF-ROOTED); explicit JSON structs.
  - `gitmoot workflow note <label> "<body>" [--author A] [--remember [--agent NAME] [--repo R]]` — requires the label to have >=1 existing job (typo guard; clear error otherwise); body capped 10KB, author capped 128; stored verbatim (document that).

### 6. Memory hook (--remember): mirror `chat remember` VERBATIM — not MemoryController
runChatRemember (internal/cli/chat.go ~423-540) is the template: memory.PreFilter → store.ObservationDedupKeys → memory.IngestKey("workflow-"+label, noteID) → InsertMemoryObservation{TrustMark: TrustLow, Provenance: fmt.Sprintf("workflow:%s#%d", label, noteID)} → autoConfirmObservationIfEnabled (honor the existing config exactly like chat remember does).
- POOL DEFAULT = SHARED (OwnerKindShared — an external coordinator has no natural private owner; shared facts join every agent's retrieval). `--agent NAME` = private-pool opt-out (must be a registered agent; error otherwise). Preserve --author in AuthorRef.
- Repo binding: when the group's jobs span exactly ONE repo, infer it; zero or multiple distinct repos → REQUIRE --repo with a clear error.
- ATOMIC: note row + observation in one transaction — --remember can never partially succeed. Store the observation id in workflow_notes.memory_observation_id.
- PreFilter rejection: fail the command with the filter's reason; no note row is written either (whole tx rolls back) — document.

### 7. Job surfaces
`gitmoot job list --workflow <label>` filter (indexed) and workflow id shown in `gitmoot job show` (and its --json). `agent ask --json` output must stay parseable (byte-clean contract).

## Tests
- Migration/back-compat: unlabeled JobPayload serializes byte-identically; ADD COLUMN DEFAULT '' leaves existing rows reading identically; legacy-schema fixture updated if the suite has one.
- EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN tests proving list/show queries use the partial index (mirror TestListQueuedJobsUsesQueuedIndex).
- CLI parse: --workflow / --workflow= on all five verbs; invalid label rejected at parse AND at enqueue; usage strings updated.
- Inheritance: an orchestrate root with WorkflowID → delegation children + every continuation kind carry it; payloadMatchesRequest distinguishes differently-grouped requests.
- workflow list/show: aggregates correct incl. per-state counts + token sums; interleaving deterministic; --limit; unknown label on show → clean empty/error; note typo-guard.
- Memory hook: shared-pool default; --agent private pool (registered check); repo inference/require; atomic rollback on PreFilter rejection; provenance format; auto-confirm honored per config; dedup keys.
- job list --workflow filter.

## Docs (same change, BOTH trees)
skills/gitmoot/references/CLI.md + WORKFLOWS.md (new section: external-coordinator workflows — the flag, the journal, --remember semantics incl. shared default + verbatim storage), SKILL.md family list if it enumerates command families (there is now a regression test for that — internal/skill/skill_test.go — make it pass honestly by documenting the new family), docs/ if applicable, website mirrors + llms regeneration.

## Build / self-check
Toolchain: /root/.local/toolchains/go1.26.4/bin/go, GOTOOLCHAIN=local, -buildvcs=false, GOCACHE=/tmp/impl843-gocache. go generate ./... (if contract/docs generation applies), go build ./..., go vet ./internal/..., go test ./internal/db/... ./internal/workflow/... ./internal/cli/... ./internal/skill/... — all green. git diff --check clean; ASCII-clean changed Go lines.

## HARD CONSTRAINTS
No git/gh. v1 scope exactly as above: NO group lifecycle control, NO web-dashboard section (follow-up). Output: decision-by-decision realization (file:line), test names, incompletes.

cases.note: Produce-stage implementation extends the shipped pipeline primitive. Verbatim instructions from jobs.id=local-ask-impl814-18c1098a7f20af82.
cases.instructions:
Implement GitHub issue #814 MVP for gitmoot: the `produce` stage — an agent stage that writes DATA (never repo/PR), codex-only, with token accounting and the result contract.

You work in /root/gitmoot-wt-814 on branch feat/814-produce-stage (current main, includes #813/#815/#816). Repo: jerryfane/gitmoot (Go). Sources of truth: issue #814, its research-panel synthesis comment, and the owner scope comment (Landlock guardrail is a SEPARATE sub-issue — NOT this PR). Do NOT run git commit/push/gh — edit files only.

## MVP SCOPE (final — codex-only)
A new agent-stage action `produce`: the agent MAY write into explicitly declared data paths; it can never touch the repo, a branch, a task worktree, or a PR. claude/kimi/kimi-cli/shell runtimes are REFUSED for produce stages fail-closed at dispatch preflight (clear error naming the runtime; the Landlock sub-issue will lift this later).

## EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED (by the coordinator, today, codex-cli 0.144.1)
- `codex exec --sandbox workspace-write --add-dir <abs-path>` allows writes into that path; without it, writes outside the roots are DENIED by the sandbox.
- CAVEAT to document: workspace-write's DEFAULT writable roots (workdir + /tmp + $TMPDIR) remain writable regardless — declared `writes:` are ADDITIVE grants, not an exhaustive allowlist. Docs must say this plainly.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (final, from panel + scope)
1. **Spec shape** (internal/pipeline/spec.go + validate.go): `action: produce` on an agent stage (pure append to the #770 Kind() dispatch — add StageKindAgentProduce). New per-stage field `writes: []string` (required for produce, >=1 entries, absolute, cleaned; REJECTED on any other stage kind). Produce REQUIRES `write: true` (widen validateMutatingStage: mutating = implement OR produce; `write: true` valid on both; a SCHEDULED pipeline requires the existing `allow_scheduled_writes` for produce too — one flag covers both mutating kinds, document that). Optional per-stage `network: true` (valid only on produce): appends `-c sandbox_workspace_write.network_access=true` (ChatSeat precedent, adapter.go) for data loops that upload (R2 etc.).
2. **Path hygiene at `gitmoot pipeline add` preflight** (internal/cli/pipeline.go, near the agent-exists warning): EvalSymlinks + Clean each write path; REJECT paths that are, contain, or are contained by: the gitmoot home, any managed repo CheckoutPath, or the path "/" and other obvious footguns. This needs store context so it lives in the CLI preflight, not pure spec validation.
3. **Request plumbing** (internal/cli/pipeline_run.go + internal/workflow/mailbox.go): produce branch in pipelineStageJobRequest (Action="produce", carry `WritablePaths []string` + the network flag on JobRequest/JobPayload, additive/omitempty); produce stages are eligible for the detached read-only worktree as cwd (add "produce" to the eligibility switch) so stray repo writes land in a disposable worktree that is never pushed; leaf contract (pipeline sender strips delegations) unchanged; NO Branch/TaskID/PR fields ever.
4. **Settle/fold**: produce folds by plain decision via foldPipelineStageOutcome (add the StageKindAgentProduce case in stageSettleOutcome mirroring the read-only agent case; NO PR guard). Reuse gitmoot_result verbatim — NO new decision token. Batch conventions go in docs: implemented=batch complete, changes_requested=partial (opt-in via success_decisions), blocked=needs human (parks with needs), skipped=no work (#815).
5. **Runtime enforcement** (internal/runtime/adapter.go + the daemon worker): mirror the ChatSeat pattern — the worker copies payload.WritablePaths (+network) onto the in-memory runtime.Agent; codexSandboxArgs appends one `--add-dir <path>` per declared path under workspace-write (and the network -c when set). Fail-closed preflights: (a) produce on a non-codex runtime → refuse at dispatch with a clear error (mirror readOnlyImplementationBlocked's shape in agent_permissions.go); (b) produce on an agent whose policy is read-only → refuse (it would run and silently write nothing); (c) danger-full-access agents: allow but the --add-dir args are harmless no-ops — document.
6. **QA hook — the adoption-critical piece**: per-stage `check: <cmd>` + `check_retries: N` (N>=0, default 0; both valid ONLY on produce stages for this MVP). Run at the MAILBOX seam after a valid result and before terminal persistence (precedents: the malformed-envelope repair loop mailbox.go:~716-724 and the #526 deterministic result checks): execute `check` via sh -c (bounded output, e.g. 8KB tail, redacted); on nonzero exit, re-deliver to the SAME session with the fenced check output appended ("Your produce output failed the deterministic check. Fix and return a fresh gitmoot_result. Check output: ..."); up to check_retries re-asks; on exhaustion the job folds failed (normal stage retry can then re-run the whole stage). Every correction turn's tokens are captured like any delivery. The check cmd runs with cwd = the stage's worktree and the SAME env; it is TRUSTED operator config (like stage cmd), not agent output.
7. **Retry reconciliation note**: when a produce STAGE retry re-runs (attempt > 0), prepend a short reconcile note to the instructions ("A previous attempt may have written partial data into your writable paths; reconcile/idempotently overwrite rather than duplicating."). Docs REQUIRE idempotent writes.
8. **Token rollup (the RFC's killer motivation, near-free)**: `pipeline show` gains a per-run token line — SumJobTokensByRoot(run.ID) (store.go:~2627) — and per-stage input/output tokens on the stage JSON where the job row has them. Label honestly (codex resumed-session edge cases contribute 0; kimi reports 0 — #659).
9. **Action vocabulary ripple** (bounded, from the panel): "produce" joins the action lists that matter for dispatch/validation — grep `"ask", "review", "implement"` and audit each (agent.go capability allowlist, engine capability gate, job_session, chat, dashboard_config, heartbeats) — but produce is valid ONLY for pipeline-sender jobs (enqueue-time check): `gitmoot agent run --type produce` etc. must NOT become a general verb in this MVP. Keep `produce` OUT of the delegation vocabulary (result.go delegation validators unchanged).
10. **Lifecycle**: gitmoot never deletes/cleans declared write dirs (operator-owned data); only its own disposable cwd worktree follows existing cleanup. Document.

## Tests
- validate: produce requires agent+prompt+write:true+writes; writes rejected elsewhere; check/check_retries/network only on produce; scheduled+produce requires allow_scheduled_writes; path-hygiene preflight rejections (home, managed checkout, symlink-resolved).
- request: produce request carries action/WritablePaths/network, no Branch/TaskID/PR; worktree-eligible; leaf strip intact.
- adapter: codexSandboxArgs emits one --add-dir per path (+network -c when set) under workspace-write; read-only produce refused; non-codex produce refused with the clear error.
- QA hook: check pass → fold proceeds; check fail → re-delivery to same session with fenced output; retries bounded; exhaustion folds failed; tokens recorded per correction turn; check output redacted/capped.
- fold: produce folds by decision incl. skipped (#815) and blocked-parks; no PR guard.
- retry note: attempt>0 instructions carry the reconcile note.
- rollup: pipeline show text+JSON token fields.
- Shell-runtime E2E note: the shell runtime is REFUSED for produce (decision 5a) — so E2E the plumbing with a codex-free path: unit-level E2E through the enqueue/settle passes with a fake enqueuer + injected job rows (existing pipeline test idioms). The coordinator will run a LIVE codex produce E2E after review.

## Docs (same change, BOTH trees)
docs/pipelines.md (produce section: shape, writes additive-to-default-roots caveat, codex-only + why, check/check_retries loop, network flag, idempotency requirement, batch decision conventions, token rollup), skills CLI.md + WORKFLOWS.md + RESULT_CONTRACT.md (batch conventions note), website pipelines-workflow.md (+ llms regen). Update the #814 issue expectations ONLY via code/docs — no gh.

## Build / self-check

## HARD CONSTRAINTS
No git/gh. Codex-only MVP — no Landlock, no claude/kimi enablement, no SkillOpt harvest expansion (follow-ups). Output when done: files changed, decision-by-decision realization (file:line), test names, incompletes.

cases.note: Skipped-stage semantics extend the shipped pipeline primitive. Verbatim instructions from jobs.id=local-ask-impl815-18c0fe8f9803f06f.
cases.instructions:
Implement GitHub issue #815 for gitmoot: a first-class "nothing to do" outcome — a `skipped` result decision that pipeline stages fold as success instead of parking the run.

You work in /root/gitmoot-wt-815 on branch feat/815-skipped-decision (based on current main, includes #800). Repo: jerryfane/gitmoot (Go). The issue and its research-panel synthesis comment (issue #815 on GitHub) are the source of truth; this spec encodes the coordinator's final design decisions. Do NOT run git commit/push/gh; edit files only. The coordinator handles git, review, and the PR.

## Problem (recap)
A pipeline stage that legitimately has no work (e.g. "no new replies today") has no honest terminal decision: `gitmoot_result` decisions are approved|changes_requested|blocked|implemented|failed; the advancer folds success only on success_decisions (default approved/implemented) and parks on everything else. So benign no-ops must lie ("implemented") or park the run.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (final — do not relitigate)
1. **Token = `skipped`** (a RESULT DECISION). Do NOT add a new stage state and do NOT touch the existing `StageSkipped` stage state (internal/pipeline/state.go:37), which keeps meaning "never ran (downstream of a halted branch)". A skipped stage folds to the EXISTING `pipeline.StageSucceeded` row state.
2. **Trusted summary marker:** when the fold sees decision==skipped, stamp the persisted stage summary with the prefix `[skipped: no work] ` followed by the agent's own summary (mirror the appendPipelineImplementPR marker mechanics, internal/cli/pipeline_run.go:~1223). Downstream needs-context (buildPipelineAgentStageContext, pipeline_run.go:~1543) then carries the honest note automatically.
3. **Default-on:** add `skipped` to BOTH `SuccessDecisionCandidates` AND `DefaultSuccessDecisions` (internal/pipeline/spec.go:25,32) — a no-op advances with zero config. This is zero-regression: no existing pipeline can emit the token today (validation rejects it).
4. **Strict-mode opt-out preserved:** if a spec sets an explicit `success_decisions` list that OMITS skipped, the stage folds failed (the author explicitly demanded real work). Document this in the spec.go comments and docs.
5. **Implement-stage bypass (REQUIRED, wedge-avoidance):** in implementStageSettleOutcome (pipeline_run.go:1151-1198), when the terminal job's decision is `skipped`, fold success IMMEDIATELY with the skip marker — never enter the fold-on-PR-opened wait. (The adjacent pre-existing wedge for decision `approved` is tracked separately as issue #817 — do NOT fix #817 in this change, keep the diff scoped, but write the skipped bypass so it clearly does not worsen it.)
6. **Gate downstream of a skipped source:** in gateStageSettleOutcome (pipeline_run.go:~1257-1298), when the SOURCE stage row is StageSucceeded and its summary carries the `[skipped: no work]` marker, fold the gate stage BLOCKED with a clear need (e.g. "source stage skipped: no PR will exist for this run") instead of waiting forever on a PR that will never come. Blocked parks the run — correct: deploy must not run after nothing merged.
7. **Coordinator ambiguity guard:** in validateAgentResult (internal/workflow/result.go:~239), REJECT a result whose decision is `skipped` AND has non-empty delegations (a coordinator that fanned out work did not "skip").
8. **Contract prose:** the regenerated contract text must scope `skipped` to "the task itself had no work to do"; explicitly say it must NOT be used by PR reviewers to mean "nothing to flag" (that is `approved`), and not alongside delegations.
9. **Non-pipeline semantics (document, don't change):** stateForDecision maps skipped→JobSucceeded via the default case (result.go:~617) — keep. Vote counts a skipped child (succeeded-state short-circuit) while quorum/verify do NOT count it as approval — leave both as-is; document the asymmetry in the contract prose ("skipped is an abstention for quorum/verify").

## Files to touch (from the panel's converged design order)

2. internal/prompts/contractgen/main.go — add skipped to the decision enum + the prose from decision 8; run `go generate ./...` so internal/prompts/contract_generated.go is regenerated and committed (there is a drift test: internal/prompts/contract_drift_test.go — update goldens as required by the generator's own workflow, do not hand-edit generated output).

4. internal/cli/pipeline_run.go foldPipelineStageOutcome (~1380) — skipped folds StageSucceeded + `[skipped: no work] ` summary prefix, honoring decision 4's strict-mode (fold path goes through the success_decisions check as today; the marker stamping is the only special-case).

Also check `gitmoot job close --decision` validates via the shared workflow.ResultDecisions set (it should pick the token up automatically — verify, don't duplicate).

cases.instructions:
Implement GitHub issue #816 for gitmoot: intra-stage progress for long-running pipeline stages.

You work in /root/gitmoot-wt-816 on branch feat/816-stage-progress (current main, includes #815). Repo: jerryfane/gitmoot (Go). Issue #816 + its research-panel synthesis comment are the source of truth; this spec fixes the coordinator's final decisions. Do NOT run git commit/push/gh — edit files only.

## DESIGN DECISIONS (final)
1. **Name:** the event kind is `progress` (the word "heartbeat" is TAKEN by the scheduled agent-heartbeat feature — never use it in identifiers or events).
2. **Storage = latest-only job_event upsert**, NOT columns on pipeline_run_stages (the advancer's "re-scan on an unchanged run makes no writes" invariant must stay intact). In internal/db/store.go add:
   - `UpsertLatestJobEvent(ctx, JobEvent)`: UPDATE job_events SET message=?, created_at=CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE job_id=? AND kind=?; INSERT when 0 rows affected. Guard against the terminal race: only write while the job row's state is 'running' (single tx; a late ticker after terminal must be a no-op).
   - `GetLatestJobEventByKind(ctx, jobID, kind)` riding the existing idx_job_events_kind_job_id covering index.
   - Bulk variant for pipeline show: fetch latest progress events for a set of job ids in ONE query.
3. **Phase 0 (read-side elapsed):** `gitmoot pipeline show <run>` renders, for each in-flight (queued/running) stage, one detail line: state + "enqueued <elapsed> ago" computed from pipeline_run_stages.started_at (BE HONEST in wording: started_at is set at enqueue, not at claim — do not call it "running for"). Add StartedAt/FinishedAt (+ the progress payload when present) to pipelineRunStageJSON (internal/cli/pipeline.go:~767) additively. Jobless gate stages have no JobID — render from StartedAt only, never assume a JobID.
4. **Phase 1 (worker-side emitter):** in the daemon job worker (internal/cli/daemon.go jobWorker.run region), ONLY for payload.Sender == workflow.PipelineJobSender jobs:
   - Wrap the runtime adapter with subprocess.TeeRunner (internal/subprocess/runner.go — it exists and preserves group-kill) feeding a mutex'd last-line tracker: keep the last non-empty line, strip ANSI/control chars, run workflow.RedactCommentText over it, hard-cap ~400 bytes, valid-UTF8-safe truncation.
   - COMPOSE with the existing cockpit tee rather than replacing it: when both are active they must share one SyncWriter'd output (io.MultiWriter under the existing SyncWriter seam) — find the cockpit tee construction (~daemon.go:5546-5597 and the adapter rebuild at ~:6600) and make ONE composition point.
   - A goroutine off the job's run context emits the upsert only after a 60s threshold, then every ~30s, carrying: elapsed since DELIVERY (claim time, the accurate clock) + the last activity line (may be empty for claude — that's fine, elapsed-only). Stop on ctx cancel (job timeout/kill included); never block or fail the job on emit errors (best-effort).
   - Threshold + interval as package constants with injectable seams for tests (no 60s sleeps in unit tests).
5. **Rendering the progress:** `pipeline show` prints the latest progress line under a running stage ("last activity <age>: <text>"); the age must be computed so a STALE progress record visibly ages (a dead daemon must not present as current liveness).
6. **Orchestrate stages (#758):** stageRow.JobID re-points along the continuation chain; mid-fan-out it references a settled coordinator, so progress may be absent/stale — render "(sub-tree running; no per-stage progress)" when the stage is an orchestrate stage and no fresh progress exists. Never treat stale/absent progress as failure.
7. **Out of scope:** tokens-so-far (claude reports usage only at exit); the external gitmoot-dashboard repo field (Phase 2 — note it in code comments as a follow-up; the dashboard can already render elapsed from the StartedAt it receives today); any change to pipeline_run_stages rows.
8. **Security:** the activity line is attacker-influenceable text rendered to operator surfaces — the strip/redact/cap in decision 4 is mandatory; NEVER inject progress text into any agent prompt.

## Files (from the panel's converged design)
- internal/db/store.go (+_test): the two/three new store funcs; no schema migration needed (job_events exists).
- internal/cli/daemon.go: the emitter + tee composition.
- internal/cli/pipeline.go: show rendering + JSON additions (funnel label logic ~:702-754, JSON ~:767).
- internal/subprocess/: should need NO changes (TeeRunner exists) — if something is genuinely missing, add minimally.
- Check the stuck-running reaper / runner_boot_id liveness (workflow/mailbox.go ~:1121) keys on the jobs row, not job_events — confirm progress writes cannot mask or trigger reaping; leave a comment where relevant.
- Docs BOTH trees: skills/gitmoot/references/CLI.md + WORKFLOWS.md (pipeline show section), docs/pipelines.md, website/docs/workflows/pipelines-workflow.md (+ regenerate website/static/llms-full.txt if the repo's flow does that).

## Tests
- store: upsert latest-only (two upserts ⇒ one row, message replaced), terminal-race guard (upsert after job terminal ⇒ no write), by-kind lookup, bulk fetch.
- emitter unit tests with injected clock/threshold: no event before threshold; events at cadence; stops on ctx cancel; last-line tracker strips ANSI + redacts + caps; empty-line handling.
- pipeline show: renders enqueued-elapsed for running stages; renders progress line with age; gate stage (no JobID) safe; orchestrate stage renders the sub-tree note; JSON additive fields present.
- cockpit-composition: when cockpit tee is active, both writers receive lines (existing cockpit tests as the model).
- Shell-runtime E2E (isolated home, model on existing pipeline E2Es): a slow shell stage (loop with echo+sleep, use SHORT injected threshold) produces a progress event whose message carries the last echoed line, and `pipeline show` output includes it.

## Build / self-check
/root/.local/toolchains/go1.26.4/bin/go, GOTOOLCHAIN=local, -buildvcs=false, temp GOCACHE (/tmp/impl816-gocache). go build ./..., go vet ./internal/..., go test ./internal/db/... ./internal/cli/... ./internal/workflow/... ./internal/subprocess/... — fix all failures. git diff --check clean; no non-ASCII in changed Go lines; race-sensitivity: run `go test -race -run 'Progress|Tee|Upsert' ./internal/cli/... ./internal/db/...` since the tracker is shared with the subprocess tee goroutine.

## HARD CONSTRAINTS
No git commit/push/gh/PR. Stay scoped to #816. When done, output: files changed, how each decision (1-8) is realized (file:line), test names, anything incomplete.

cases.instructions:
Implement GitHub issue #813 for gitmoot: `source:` PR-binding for pipeline REVIEW stages — make [implement] → [review the PR] → [gate: pr_merged] first-class.

You work in /root/gitmoot-wt-813 on branch feat/813-review-pr-binding (current main). Repo: jerryfane/gitmoot (Go). Issue #813 + its research-panel synthesis + the owner scope comment are the source of truth; this spec fixes the coordinator's final decisions. Do NOT run git commit/push/gh — edit files only.

## SCOPE (final)
- REVIEW-stage binding ONLY. Orchestrate-stage binding is explicitly DEFERRED (panel consensus) — do not touch orchestrate dispatch.
- `merge: auto` (the owner-approved scope extension) is a FOLLOW-UP PR — here you implement the human-merge default: the suppression guard that makes PR-bound pipeline review jobs report-only. Design the guard so the auto mode can later hang off the same seam (a clear single decision point, commented).

## DESIGN DECISIONS (final, from the panel)
1. **Spec/validation** (internal/pipeline/spec.go + validate.go): permit `source: <stage-id>` on `action: review` agent stages. Validation mirrors the gate's checks (validate.go:~352-360): source must be in the stage's own Needs AND reference an `action: implement` stage. ADD explicit rejection of `source:` on every other non-gate stage kind (shell, ask-agent, implement, orchestrate) — today a stray source is silently ignored; make it a validation error (documented tightening). Update the spec.go Source doc comment (:~126-130).
2. **Binding resolution at enqueue** (internal/cli/pipeline_run.go, the enqueue pass ~:771-808): for a source-bound review stage, resolve the binding from the SOURCE STAGE ROW's JobID → store.GetJob → workflow.ParseJobPayload → {PullRequest, HeadSHA, Branch, TaskID, LeadAgent}. Do NOT parse the summary marker (the gate's summary parse carries only the PR number; the structured stamp on the implement job's payload is authoritative — FinalizeImplementation sets PullRequest/HeadSHA/Branch, daemon.go:~7036-7068). Thread the binding into pipelineStageJobRequest (:~340) as a new parameter; set PullRequest + HeadSHA (+ Branch/TaskID per decision 4) on the review branch of the request builder. The binding must be DETERMINISTIC across advancer re-scans (the folded source job payload is immutable) to preserve idempotent enqueue.
3. **No-PR source handling — never wait, never wedge:** implementStageSettleOutcome already guarantees the stamp is final before the source folds success. The ONLY PR-less successes are (a) the permanent no-op (`advance_skipped_no_pr`) and (b) a `skipped` decision (#815, summary marker `[skipped: no work]`). For (b) the GATE already folds blocked (shipped in #815); mirror that for a source-bound REVIEW stage: when the source succeeded with PR==0, fold the review stage BLOCKED at enqueue time with a clear need ("source stage produced no PR; nothing to review") — do NOT dispatch an unbound review and do NOT hold the enqueue.
4. **Worktree at the PR head (critical — wrong-tree hazard):** the stage's read-only worktree is currently allocated detached at main's tip (allocatePipelineStageReadOnlyWorktree, pipeline_run.go:~127-165, passes "" as ref). For a bound review stage pass the bound HeadSHA as the AllocateReadOnlyWorktree base ref (the ref param exists, workflow/worktree.go:~309-329; the SHA is resolvable via the shared object DB). Then decide the checkout-validation route empirically: EITHER include TaskID in the binding so defaultCheckout runs validateReviewCheckout (requires worktree HEAD == payload.HeadSHA; detached is fine — it checks clean+head, never branch), OR leave TaskID empty and ensure the PR-less-arm/branchless escape validates the pinned detached worktree. READ daemon.go:~7319-7367 + resyncReviewHead (~:7618-7677) carefully: the FAILURE MODE to prevent is resyncReviewHead silently re-targeting a detached worktree to main's tip. Add a regression test asserting the review job's checkout HEAD == the bound HeadSHA.
5. **Lifecycle suppression guard (the panel's CRITICAL finding):** with PullRequest>0 the review job's terminal advance would run the NATIVE review lifecycle — dispatchFix on changes_requested (engine.go:~1632-1651) and runMergeGate on approved (engine.go:~1653+, can MERGE the PR) — violating "the pipeline never merges itself". Add a guard at the top of AdvanceJob's review arm: when the job's payload Sender == workflow.PipelineJobSender, record a job event (e.g. `pipeline_review_report_only`) and return nil — the pipeline advancer owns the fold; the verdict still folds by decision and still auto-posts as a PR comment (daemon.go:~7932-7950 — keep that, it is the visible win). Precedents: the mailbox pipeline-sender delegations strip (mailbox.go:~814-850) and job_recovery's pipeline-sender refusal (job_recovery.go:~180). Comment the guard as the single seam where a future `merge: auto` policy will branch.
6. **Duplicate fan-out:** when a spec declares a source-bound review stage downstream of an implement stage, set SkipNativeReviewFanout on THAT implement stage's JobRequest (field exists, mailbox.go:~175; honored engine.go:~912 and the finalizer write-ahead daemon.go:~6987-6991) so the PR does not ALSO get native reviewer fan-out. Only when the spec declares it — otherwise byte-identical behavior.
7. **Fold semantics unchanged:** the review stage folds by decision via success_decisions exactly like any agent stage — changes_requested parks the run before the human merge gate (that is the point). No new decisions.
8. **ReviewRound:** leave empty on the stage job (with the sender guard the native round bookkeeping is bypassed; latestReviewRound skips empty rounds).

## Files
internal/pipeline/spec.go, internal/pipeline/validate.go (+tests), internal/cli/pipeline_run.go (+ enqueue/settle tests), internal/workflow/engine.go (guard + test), internal/workflow/mailbox.go ONLY if a payload marker is genuinely needed (prefer sender-based guard; review children do not exist in this scope since orchestrate binding is deferred), internal/cli/daemon.go only if the checkout-validation route requires it. Docs BOTH trees: docs/pipelines.md, skills/gitmoot/references/{CLI,WORKFLOWS}.md, website/docs/workflows/pipelines-workflow.md (+ llms regen if applicable) — document source: on review stages, the report-only rule, the no-PR blocked fold, and SkipNativeReviewFanout behavior.

## Tests
- validate: source accepted on review stage (in Needs + implement source); rejected on shell/ask/implement/orchestrate; rejected when not in Needs or wrong source kind.
- enqueue binding: request carries PullRequest+HeadSHA from the implement payload stamp; deterministic across two advancer scans; retry of the source re-resolves from the succeeded attempt's job.
- no-PR source: review stage folds blocked with the need (both the advance_skipped_no_pr case and the #815 skipped case).
- guard: a pipeline-sender review job with PR>0 and decision changes_requested does NOT dispatch a fix job; with approved does NOT run the merge gate; event recorded; a NON-pipeline review job is untouched (regression).
- worktree: bound review stage's checkout HEAD == HeadSHA (no resync-to-main).
- fan-out: implement stage request carries SkipNativeReviewFanout only when the spec has a downstream source-bound review stage.
- Shell-runtime E2E (isolated home, model on pipeline_gate_stage_e2e_test.go): implement stage stamps a PR → review stage job payload carries PR+SHA → changes_requested parks the run with NO fix job and NO merge-gate evaluation → (separate case) approved review + merged PR lets the gate fold.

## Build / self-check
Same toolchain rules (/root/.local/toolchains/go1.26.4/bin/go, GOTOOLCHAIN=local, -buildvcs=false, temp GOCACHE /tmp/impl813-gocache). go build ./..., go vet ./internal/..., go test ./internal/pipeline/... ./internal/cli/... ./internal/workflow/... — fix everything. git diff --check clean; ASCII-clean changed lines.

## HARD CONSTRAINTS
No git/gh. Scoped to #813 core (no merge:auto, no orchestrate binding). Output when done: files changed, decision-by-decision realization (file:line), test names, incompletes.

cases.instructions:
FEATURE: "Fact galaxy" (approved design, V1 HYBRID variant only) for the dashboard's Learning → Knowledge page. Replace the FACT COLUMN inside each repo band with a force-settled constellation of fact nodes whose layout forces are their wiki-links. The sub-cluster / cluster / agent COLUMNS stay exactly as today (that is what V1 Hybrid means). Single-page vanilla-JS codebase.

ARMOR (mandatory): do NOT git commit and do NOT git push — gitmoot commits after you finish. Work only inside your task worktree. Keep the diff scoped to: web/dist/index.html, datasource.go, fakefeed.go (and an api/contract test file only if one already covers Edge fields).

READ FIRST (in this order):
1. The DESIGN DOC — it is the spec, read its full text: "/tmp/claude-0/-root-gitmoot/66fbda3c-4765-4cdd-9c48-15d495173823/scratchpad/design-zip/design/Fact Galaxy.dc.html" (read the HTML text content; it contains the layout/physics/score/interaction cards and V1-vs-V2 tradeoffs). Also inspect "/tmp/claude-0/-root-gitmoot/66fbda3c-4765-4cdd-9c48-15d495173823/scratchpad/design-zip/design/galaxy.js" — a WORKING prototype of the two-phase layout + rendering used to produce the mockups. Port its approach and constants; adapt them to this page's existing helpers rather than pasting wholesale.
2. Current knowledge code in web/dist/index.html: _knowBuild (payload -> nodes/links), _knowLayoutClusteredFlat / _knowLayoutClusteredSplit (band geometry, level x-positions, min-pitch sweeps — added recently, keep all of it), _knowFit (width-anchored fit + top-anchor + pan), the knowledge nodeCanvasObject + onRenderFramePre (canvas render, constant screen-px labels), _knowEnsureGutterClear, renderKnowledge (data signature -> deterministic static layout -> freeze), _knowHash/_knowRand (seeded determinism helpers).
3. cmd/gitmoot-dashboard-dev + fakefeed.go (the fixture the dev harness serves on :8099) and datasource.go (the Edge contract).

CONTRACT (the real backend emit lands in a separate gitmoot-repo PR; this PR defines the contract + fixture):
- datasource.go: Edge gains `Score float64` with json tag "score,omitempty". Document a new edge kind "link" = fact<->fact wiki link, emitted once per pair (undirected), score in (0,1].
- fakefeed.go: emit plausible "link" edges in the knowledge fixture (scores spread 0.15..0.95; mostly in-cluster pairs, several cross-cluster, at least one cross-repo pair) so the dev harness demonstrates the galaxy. Keep the -knowledge-flat fixture path working.
- The UI must be GRACEFUL with zero link edges (live server does not emit them yet): constellation still lays out (facts become loose dust), the link-count chip shows 0, toggles still render.

SPEC (from the approved design — implement exactly):
1. TWO-PHASE DETERMINISTIC LAYOUT per band: phase 1 = cluster centroids separate (mutual repulsion + cohesion pulling a parent's sub-cluster centroids together), phase 2 = facts pack around their cluster centroid with a collision radius so dots never pile up; wiki-links act as springs (spring strength and rest length driven by score: strong links short, weak links long). Fixed iteration count, seeded from the data signature (reuse _knowHash/_knowRand style seeding) — SAME DATA => IDENTICAL SKY. Run to convergence ONCE at layout time, then freeze positions (fx/fy pinned). No live physics, no per-poll jitter — matching the page's existing static-determinism contract.
2. CONTAINMENT: every fact stays INSIDE its repo band rect (soft wall force + final clamp). Band height derives from constellation area (fact count + clump spread) instead of a fact-column pitch; bands stay unequal and stacked; the existing width-anchored _knowFit + pan behavior is preserved; the count chip + legend stay pinned.
3. SCORE => VISUAL WEIGHT: link render alpha = 0.05 + 0.42 * pow(clamp((s - 0.2) / 0.75, 0, 1), 1.4); width = 0.5 + 1.15*s px (screen px / scale like other constant-size strokes). Below s~0.2 links fade toward invisible. Kind scaling: in-clump cyan x1.0, cross-clump indigo x~0.4, cross-repo violet x~0.4. Cross-REPO links are OFF by default behind their own toggle; when on (or when an endpoint fact is hovered) they draw as dim violet hairlines to a STUB at the band edge — never pull a fact out of its band (exclude them from layout forces entirely).
4. MEMBERSHIP FANS (fact -> its sub-cluster/cluster node): keep them at low density; when a band has more than ~40 facts, fans become hover-only (the density mock shows them collapsed). Fans keep converging on the correct node (sub-cluster if the fact's cluster has a parent, else the cluster).
5. OWNERSHIP FIX (part of this PR): the client-side per-cluster aggregation of fact `owner` edges must roll up to the TOP-LEVEL cluster — walk parentId to the root — so sub-clusters NEVER connect to the agents rail. Ownership hairlines leave only top-level hubs.
6. TOGGLES + CHIP: a "fact links" toggle (default ON) and a "cross-repo links" toggle (default OFF) styled like the page's existing chips, plus a "N fact links" count chip next to the showing-summary. Legend gains: "fact link (weight = score)" and "cross-clump"; add "cross-repo" entry only when that toggle is on.
7. HOVER-FOCUS (the density workhorse): hovering a fact lights ONLY its link neighborhood (linked facts + those links at full weight) plus its own membership fan and its fact-key label; everything else dims (reuse the page's existing selection-dimming pattern). Clicking a cluster/sub-cluster node isolates its member facts + their links (extend the existing cluster-select adjacency). Escape/blank-click clears, as today.
8. LABELS: facts stay unlabeled dots at rest; hover shows the fact key (small mono label near the dot). The 6 highest-DEGREE facts per band (by link count) get a small persistent label. Constant screen-px font sizes like every other label on the page.
9. UNCHANGED: the mobile accordion path, the non-clustered fallback layout, the Skills tab, every other page, and all recently-added behaviors (min-pitch sweeps for the cluster/sub columns, equal-thirds level spacing, tint geometry, gutter nudge with padR respect).
10. Node visual language: inherit the page's existing fact/cluster/agent colors + glow (facts #6ec6ff, general facts amber, superseded grey ring, sub-clusters #a99ac8, clusters #cabff2, agents #cdbdf6, ownership #c9e98a); clump composition should read like the product's Galaxy page (clumps + dust, additive bloom) — see uploads/galaxy-ref.png in the design folder.

VERIFY before declaring done:
- go build ./... && go vet ./... && go test ./... all green in the worktree.
- Run the dev harness (go run ./cmd/gitmoot-dashboard-dev, port :8099) and confirm via curl that the page serves and the fixture emits link edges. (Visual verification is done by the coordinator after you finish — but make sure the JS parses: node --check is not applicable to inline HTML, so at minimum load the page HTML and grep your new function names.)
- Confirm determinism: the layout depends only on the data signature (no Date.now/Math.random without the seeded helper).

Return decision "implemented" with a summary of what changed; list any spec point you had to interpret. Do NOT commit or push.

cases.instructions:
RESEARCH BRIEF — 3 accuracy levers for an OfficeQA grounded-numeric-QA agent.

CONTEXT:
- Task: answer hard numeric questions over U.S. Treasury Bulletin documents — 697 parsed TEXT files (~383 MB) of dense financial tables (markdown-ish text, columns, footnotes).
- Model: MiniMax M2.7 — WEAK and FIXED (cannot change; server-enforced grader). Harness: codex. NO internet.
- Scoring: 1% relative-error tolerance. ALL graded tasks are HARD, multi-step.
- The agent is a Python MCP server that retrieves docs, parses tables, extracts numbers, and computes. Current HARD-task accuracy ~21%; field winners ~60-65%.
- Diagnosed from real failure traces: most failures are "couldn't get the right number in front of the model," not core reasoning.

HARD CONSTRAINTS for any proposed solution:
- GENERAL mechanisms only — NO task-specific tuning / no overfitting to known questions.
- DETERMINISTIC where possible (weak model => do work in code, not prompt).
- Code-level + ZERO-CONFIG: must bind on the grader with no env vars/flags (only shipped defaults run).
- Dependency-light: the server currently ships dependency-free; adding a dependency must be justified AND bundleable into the submission (no network at grader time).

FOR EACH LEVER below: give (a) the concrete problem, (b) the most RECENT / SOTA / efficient / elegant solution, with WHY it fits our constraints, (c) a specific implementable proposal for a Python MCP retrieval/table server, (d) dependency + risk notes, (e) cite recent sources. Prioritize LEVER 1.

LEVER 1 — RETRIEVAL RELIABILITY (biggest bucket, ~10/22 addressable failures):
The agent often can't FIND the right table. The bundled `search_cli` (BM25-ish over 697 text files) is SLOW (10-90 s/call), HANGS, returns EMPTY ("token count 0"), or surfaces the WRONG files. The agent falls back to it (raw shell) when the indexed MCP `search_corpus` isn't used. Research the fastest + most reliable retrieval over a FIXED ~700-doc / ~400 MB financial-table TEXT corpus: BM25 vs dense embeddings vs hybrid; sub-second build/query; why a query returns empty and how to prevent it; reranking the correct table to the top; handling numeric/entity/date/table-name queries over financial tables; keeping it dependency-light + offline.

LEVER 2 — TABLE COLUMN / ROW / BASIS SELECTION (~6 failures):
When the right table is open, the model picks the WRONG cell — an intermediate sub-aggregate instead of the labeled total, the wrong adjacent column, or it mixes fiscal-year vs calendar-year basis. Research SOTA deterministic table-cell disambiguation: resolving nested/merged column headers, detecting "total" vs sub-aggregate columns, matching a requested basis (fiscal/calendar/month-end/weekly), column-type & semantic header inference for financial tables. Elegant + general.

LEVER 3 — MULTI-STEP NUMERIC REASONING (~6 failures, model-limited):
4-5 step chains (forecast a series, convert currency, take a ratio, geometric mean, CPI-adjust) — the weak model can't finish them reliably even with the data (some were one step from correct). Research SOTA for robust multi-step numeric QA with a WEAK model: program-of-thought / code generation, task decomposition, self-verification / consistency checks, deterministic computation scaffolds — that recover multi-step accuracy WITHOUT overfitting to specific chains.
